title: Bridge Essay: Paracingulate Sulcus Morphology and Hallucinations in Clinical and Nonclinical Groups.
concept: Voice-hearing — auditory verbal hallucinations on a continuum, inner speech misattribution, source monitoring, corollary discharge.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Voice-hearing — auditory verbal hallucinations on a continuum, inner speech misattribution, source monitoring, corollary discharge.

**Paracingulate Sulcus Morphology and Hallucinations in Clinical and Nonclinical Groups.** - Schizophr Bull (2019). DOI: 10.1093/schbul/sby157. PMID: 30380115. Hallucinations are a characteristic symptom of psychotic mental health conditions that are also experienced by many individuals without a clinical diagnosis. Hallucinations in schizophrenia have been linked to differences in the length of the paracingulate sulcus (PCS), a structure in the medial prefrontal cortex which has previously been associated with the ability to differentiate perceived and imagined information.
